Patch Set 1: Fails

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.3/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/171311d0-9476-11e2-ba8c-52540035b04c).  Ran 14 tests.  1 tests failed: sanity.

Patch Set 1:

Looking at this, the patch failed test54c.  It's very possible I'm missing something, but I don't see how this patch could've caused 54c to fail as it did.

Is it possible for someone to examine this and see if there's a reason I'm missing this would cause 54c to fail?

Here's the test:
---
test_54c() {
        [ $PARALLEL == "yes" ] && skip "skip parallel run" && return
        tfile="$DIR/f54c"
        tdir="$DIR/d54c"
        loopdev="$DIR/loop54c"

        find_loop_dev
        [ -z "$LOOPNUM" ] && echo "couldn't find empty loop device" && return
        mknod $loopdev b 7 $LOOPNUM
        echo "make a loop file system with $tfile on $loopdev ($LOOPNUM)..."
        dd if=/dev/zero of=$tfile bs=`page_size` seek=1024 count=1 > /dev/null
        losetup $loopdev $tfile || error "can't set up $loopdev for $tfile"
        mkfs.ext2 $loopdev || error "mke2fs on $loopdev"
        test_mkdir -p $tdir
        mount -t ext2 $loopdev $tdir || error "error mounting $loopdev on $tdir"
        dd if=/dev/zero of=$tdir/tmp bs=`page_size` count=30 || error "dd write"
        df $tdir
        dd if=$tdir/tmp of=/dev/zero bs=`page_size` count=30 || error "dd read"
        $UMOUNT $tdir
        losetup -d $loopdev
        rm $loopdev
}
run_test 54c "block device works in lustre ====================="
---

And here's the LBUG on one of the clients that I believe led to the test failing:
---
06:21:54:Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: == sanity test 54c: block device works in lustre ======================= 06:21:47 (1364044907)
06:21:54:LustreError: 31629:0:(lcommon_cl.c:794:ccc_io_update_iov()) ASSERTION( cio->cui_tot_nrsegs >= cio->cui_nrsegs ) failed: tot_nrsegs: 0, nrsegs: 1
06:21:54:LustreError: 31629:0:(lcommon_cl.c:794:ccc_io_update_iov()) LBUG
---

Thoughts?
Uploaded patch set 2.
Uploaded patch set 3.

Patch Set 3:

Sorry about the double push - I received a warning about my commit message subject being too long and thought my push had failed.

This reflects the change described in previous comments.

Patch Set 2:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/14295/

Patch Set 3:

Patrick,
Regarding your test failure it looks like known bug LU-2910 to me.  A fix went in after your push.  You need to rebase and repush on the latest master to pick up recent fixes.

Patch Set 3:

Bob,

I just did this:
---
*paf@relbld12 lustre-master $ git stash
Saved working directory and index state WIP on master: b882999 LU-2030 obdclass: Lustre returns EINTR when SA_RESTART is set
HEAD is now at b882999 LU-2030 obdclass: Lustre returns EINTR when SA_RESTART is set
*paf@relbld12 lustre-master $ git rebase master
Current branch master is up to date.
---
So I think I'm already up to date.  I tried a merge with master before the rebase and it also told me I was already up to date.
---
*paf@relbld12 lustre-master $ git merge master
Already up-to-date.
---

We'll see if it fails the same test with the updated patch.  I don't believe I did any re-basing or other updating before making the new patch set this morning.

Uploaded patch set 7.

Patch Set 7:

This is changed to reflect offline comments from Cory Spitz:
if (likely(!OBD_FAIL_CHECK(OBD_FAIL_LOCK_STATE_WAIT_INTR)))
line is about error injection and will not ever be part of the normal line of execution.

Therefore, I've changed back to replacing EINTR with ERESTARTSYS, rather than changing the return value to ERESTARTSYS in the particular case.

I've also corrected the typo in the commit message - this is associated with LU-3020, not 2030.

Patch Set 7:

Sorry, my last comment should read "and will always be part of normal execution", IE, never false, so the if(!signal_pending()) will always be executed.

commit.subject: LU-3020 obdclass: Lustre returns EINTR when SA_RESTART is set
commit.message:
LU-3020 obdclass: Lustre returns EINTR when SA_RESTART is set

When Lustre is in a read or write system call and receives a
SIGALRM, it currently returns EINTR at this location. This is
problematic because it prevents the system call from being restarted
if SA_RESTART is set in the handler.

This patch changes behavior in this location to return ERESTARTSYS
when a signal is found.

Signed-off-by: Patrick Farrell <paf@cray.com>
Change-Id: I26e24b8e8e325c5b0bd7d5d20fa97e2180c12263
